demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care facility

contact_nok.relationship: facility nursing staff (informant present at the call)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I just keep ending up on the floor for no reason at all, and now my knee is incredibly swollen and sore. (Facility nursing staff on the line: she's had three falls this week, the last one today, and she won't put weight on the leg now.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: She fell again today while turning, hurt her knee, and the falls are happening more and more — the staff want advice before the next in-hours period.

symptom_narrative.onset: the knee pain came on today after a fall while turning; the falls themselves have been happening over the past week or so
symptom_narrative.duration: third fall in one week; knee swollen and sore since today
symptom_narrative.character: she describes just finding herself on the floor without warning; the knee is swollen and aching
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the knee is incredibly swollen and sore and she is frightened of falling again
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right knee; and a general unsteadiness on her feet
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the falls are becoming more frequent; the knee pain is constant since the fall
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting and keeping the weight off the knee; an ice pack the staff gave her
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to stand or turn; she feels her legs give way
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: her eyesight isn't as good as it used to be; her legs feel weak, especially the thighs, so she can't catch herself
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: two earlier falls this same week, and unsteadiness that the staff say has been getting worse
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally gets around with a four-wheeled walker; today she is refusing to stand or weight-bear because of the knee pain and fear of falling

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: gets around with a four-wheeled walker and has been unsteady on her feet
an overactive bladder that she takes a tablet for
trouble sleeping that she takes a night-time tablet for

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a sleeping tablet taken in the evening

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility; nursing staff are present and made the call on her behalf; her room has a lot of furniture in it and her walking frame has been playing up

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: readings read out by facility nursing staff, not a formal clinician-supervised postural blood-pressure protocol

objective_data_offered.value: swelling over the front of the right knee with an ice pack held to it; she is sitting in a wheelchair and looks anxious
staff describe an unsteady, wide-based, slow shuffling walk that has been getting worse, and that she trips over things in her cluttered room; the brakes on her four-wheeled walker are faulty
she is refusing to put any weight on the leg now because of the knee pain and fear of falling
